issues.title: Epic: Phase 3 — Parts requests and waiting-for-parts operational flow
issues.description:
## Outcome
Deliver the next feature block after lifecycle core: full parts request flow under work orders so blocked jobs are explicit, actionable, and auditable.

## Why now
Current system has lifecycle control but no dedicated parts-request model. This creates a blind spot for the highest-friction real-world state: jobs paused pending parts.

## Scope of this epic
- parts request domain model and transition rules,
- persistence tables + audit trail,
- APIs for create/update/receive/substitute/cancel/return,
- work-order UI for parts operations,
- dashboard waiting-for-parts queue expansion,
- automated verification scenarios,
- runbook/docs/rollback guidance.

## Epic-level acceptance
- [ ] A user can create parts requests directly under a work order.
- [ ] A user can mark receipt/substitution/return/cancel with visible history.
- [ ] Waiting-for-parts jobs are visible with aging signal and next action.
- [ ] `npm run verify` and `npm run verify:render` remain green after rollout.

## Suggested execution order
1. PRT-1 -> PRT-2 -> PRT-3
2. PRT-4 -> PRT-5 -> PRT-6
3. PRT-7 -> PRT-8

issues.title: PRT-1 Domain: parts request state machine and invariants
issues.description:
## Context
The domain packet requires explicit parts states and traceable transitions. We need deterministic business rules before storage/API/UI.

## Scope
1. Add domain module for parts request statuses and transition map (e.g. `requested`, `ordered`, `received`, `substituted`, `cancelled`, `returned`).
2. Define guarded transitions and invalid-transition errors.
3. Encode invariants: non-negative qty/cost, immutable key facts after terminal states unless correction path used.
4. Provide Russian status labels and next-action hints.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Domain module exposes canonical statuses and allowed transitions.
- [ ] Invalid transitions fail with explicit machine-parseable error codes/messages.
- [ ] Status labels are Russian and stable.

## Verification
- `npm test` with new domain-focused tests for happy-path and invalid transitions.

issues.title: PRT-2 Persistence: parts request tables, purchase actions, and history audit
issues.description:
## Context
Phase 3 requires persistent linkage between work orders and parts requests, plus supplier-side purchase actions and history.

## Scope
1. Add migration(s) for:
   - `work_order_parts_requests`,
   - `parts_purchase_actions` (supplier-side),
   - `work_order_parts_history` (status and key field changes).
2. Add indexes for active queue and work-order detail lookups.
3. Extend seed data with at least one waiting-for-parts and one substitution example.
4. Keep migration/idempotency guarantees and rollback notes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New tables exist with referential integrity to work orders.
- [ ] History captures each meaningful parts state change.
- [ ] Seed fixtures include realistic parts states.

## Verification
- `npm test` (persistence + migration tests)
- `npm run verify`

issues.title: PRT-3 API: work-order parts request endpoints with strict validation
issues.description:
## Context
Operators need direct API contracts for parts requests and supplier actions to support UI and external automation.

## Scope
1. Add endpoints under work-order context, for example:
   - `GET /api/v1/work-orders/:id/parts-requests`
   - `POST /api/v1/work-orders/:id/parts-requests`
   - `PATCH /api/v1/work-orders/:id/parts-requests/:requestId`
   - `POST /api/v1/work-orders/:id/parts-requests/:requestId/purchase-actions`
2. Add deterministic input validation and normalized error payloads.
3. Enforce role policy for mutating endpoints.
4. Keep authz coverage aligned with existing token-role baseline.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API covers create/update/receive/substitute/cancel/return flows.
- [ ] Validation rejects malformed or unsafe payloads with stable contracts.
- [ ] Unauthorized mutations return 403 with standard response shape.

## Verification
- `npm test` with new API tests
- `npm run verify`

issues.title: PRT-4 Service flow: lifecycle gating between waiting_parts and resumable work
issues.description:
## Context
Parts availability should drive work-order readiness. Today status changes are manual and can drift from actual parts state.

## Scope
1. Add service-level orchestration that derives/guards work-order status transitions based on unresolved parts requests.
2. Define deterministic rule: unresolved blocking parts force `waiting_parts` (or block conflicting transitions); resolving required parts allows continuation.
3. Write lifecycle + parts history entries in one transaction boundary.
4. Keep explicit correction path with reason capture when overriding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Work order cannot be incorrectly advanced while required parts remain unresolved.
- [ ] Resolving required parts enables progression without manual data repair.
- [ ] Every automatic or guarded transition is auditable.

## Verification
- `npm test` with service integration tests
- `npm run verify`

issues.title: PRT-5 UI: parts management section in /work-orders/:id
issues.description:
## Context
Operators currently cannot manage parts flow from the work-order workspace.

## Scope
1. Add Russian parts-management panel to `/work-orders/:id`:
   - create parts request,
   - set supplier/order metadata,
   - mark received/substituted/cancelled/returned,
   - capture reason/comment.
2. Show current parts state summary and next action.
3. Keep error messages actionable and field-specific.
4. Preserve existing lifecycle section behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parts actions are operable from the same work-order page.
- [ ] UI reflects current parts state and history without API-only workarounds.
- [ ] No regression in existing lifecycle controls.

## Verification
- `npm test` with page-flow tests
- `npm run verify`

issues.title: PRT-6 Dashboard: waiting-for-parts queue with aging and next action
issues.description:
## Context
The dashboard has queue counts but lacks full parts-driven operational visibility (aging + actionable detail) for blocked jobs.

## Scope
1. Expand dashboard payload and UI with waiting-for-parts detail rows:
   - work-order code,
   - pending parts count,
   - oldest pending age,
   - next action hint.
2. Add deterministic sorting by urgency/age.
3. Keep existing summary invariants (summary count equals queue length).
4. Ensure mobile layout remains overflow-safe.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Waiting-for-parts queue is operationally actionable, not count-only.
- [ ] Aging signal and ordering make blocked jobs obvious.
- [ ] Mobile and desktop render without horizontal overflow regressions.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run smoke`
- targeted Playwright viewport checks at 390px and desktop.

issues.title: PRT-7 Verification: end-to-end parts scenario and non-destructive deploy checks
issues.description:
## Context
Phase 3 needs first-class verification paths equivalent to booking/walk-in/lifecycle to remain agent-operable and regression-resistant.

## Scope
1. Add deterministic parts scenario script covering:
   - request creation,
   - waiting-for-parts block,
   - receipt/substitution,
   - resumed lifecycle progression.
2. Add non-destructive remote mode behavior for deployed URL.
3. Integrate scenario into `verify` and `verify:render` gates with structured JSON output.
4. Add focused tests for scenario helpers and failure diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local gate validates parts flow end-to-end automatically.
- [ ] Deploy gate validates read-only parts expectations on production URL.
- [ ] Failures are machine-parseable and actionable.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run verify`
- `npm run verify:render`

issues.title: PRT-8 Docs/Ops: parts flow runbook, acceptance mapping, rollback path
issues.description:
## Context
New feature behavior must be discoverable and recoverable without hidden knowledge.

## Scope
1. Update packet docs and runbook with parts domain rules, APIs, UI routes, and scenario commands.
2. Add acceptance mapping from docs to implemented tests/scenarios.
3. Document rollback and data-repair procedure for incorrect parts status or supplier event entry.
4. Update `STATUS.md` and `PLANS.md` to reflect the new active/completed plan.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Operator and agent can run and verify parts flow from docs only.
- [ ] Rollback/recovery instructions are explicit and tested at least once.
- [ ] Status/plans remain compact and current.

## Verification
- `npm run verify`
- `npm run verify:render`
- doc references resolve and commands execute as written.
